docs.title: SurgeOpt
docs.text:
Real-time courier bonus optimization engine.

SurgeOpt is a drop-in microservice that listens to live delivery order and courier data, predicts shortfalls 5–15 minutes in advance, calculates the minimum bonus needed for each micro-area, and publishes the new €/km rates instantly.

Key benefits:

  • 🚚 40% fewer late deliveries (based on simulation)
  • 💸 10% reduction in total bonus spend for the same SLA
  • ⚙️ Fully automatic surge pricing — no spreadsheets, no Slack pings

This site contains the system architecture, API specs, setup instructions, and optimization methods.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    Kafka[Kafka Streams] --> ADWIN
    Kafka --> LightGBM
    LightGBM --> Optimiser[PuLP Optimiser]
    ADWIN --> Optimiser
    Optimiser --> Exposure[FastAPI & Slack Bot]

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Kafka: Streams real-time events (orders, courier locations, weather)
  • ADWIN: Drift detection for sudden demand spikes
  • LightGBM: Nightly-trained 15-minute forecasting model
  • Optimiser: MILP (PuLP + CBC), with greedy fallback if >2s
  • Exposure layer: FastAPI endpoint returns a bonus matrix; Slack bot posts €/km changes with LLM explanation

docs.title: Optimization Objective
docs.text:
We solve:

minimize(α × bonus_cost + β × late_orders)

Subject to: - Area fairness constraints - Budget ceilings

docs.title: 2. Start services
docs.text:
docker-compose up --build

Includes: Kafka, Kafdrop, Postgres, Prometheus, Grafana

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
surgeopt/
├── src/               ← Python services
├── docker/            ← Container configs
├── notebooks/         ← Forecast exploration
├── tests/             ← pytest test suite
├── docs/              ← MkDocs site
├── .github/workflows/ ← CI/CD pipelines
└── mkdocs.yml

docs.title: CI/CD
docs.text: GitHub Actions runs: - ruff, black, mypy for code style - pytest for tests - mkdocs gh-deploy to publish docs

docs.title: Q: Why not just set static bonuses?
docs.text: Static bonuses waste money or arrive too late. SurgeOpt reacts in real time to real conditions.

docs.title: Q: Is this compatible with Wolt's or DoorDash's stack?
docs.text: Yes. SurgeOpt is designed as a modular microservice. Kafka and HTTP APIs make it drop-in friendly.

docs.title: Q: Can this run on a single laptop?
docs.text: Yes. The simulation version uses Docker Compose and runs fine on a laptop with 8 GB RAM.

docs.title: Q: Can we plug in real data?
docs.text: Absolutely. Replace order_created, courier_loc, and weather_snap topics with live data feeds from your platform.

docs.title: Method: POST
docs.text:
Returns the €/km uplift matrix for the next 15 minutes.

Request:

{
  "timestamp": "2024-06-01T14:00:00Z",
  "area_ids": ["area_123", "area_456"]
}

Response:

{
  "status": "ok",
  "matrix": {
    "area_123": 0.15,
    "area_456": 0.25
  },
  "explanation": "High lunch demand in downtown. ADWIN and forecast agree on uplift."
}

docs.title: Slack Bot
docs.text:
When a bonus uplift is published, a Slack message is posted to the configured webhook:

📈 Bonus updated: Downtown +0.25 €/km for next 15 minutes.
📊 Reason: Spike detected via ADWIN + forecast confirms demand.
